name: OpenWeatherMap MCP Server
what_it_does: Official OpenWeatherMap MCP server enabling AI agents to access weather data — current conditions, forecasts, historical data, air quality, and weather alerts for any location worldwide.
use_cases: Getting current weather conditions for location-aware agent decisions
Fetching weather forecasts for logistics and planning agents
Accessing historical weather data for analysis agents
Checking air quality and UV index for health-aware agents
Weather-based automation triggering in agent workflows
not_for: Ultra-precise meteorological data (use specialized weather services)
Aviation weather (use weather.gov/NOAA for METAR data)
best_when: An agent needs current or forecast weather data for any location — planning, logistics, outdoor activities, or environmental context.
avoid_when: You need aviation-grade or ultra-precise meteorological data.

auth.notes: Single API key via OWM_API_KEY. No scope granularity. API key is appended to requests as a query parameter.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 60 calls/min, 1M calls/month for current weather and forecast
pricing.notes: Very generous free tier for most agent use cases. Paid tiers add historical data, minute forecasts, alerts.

performance.rate_limits: 60 calls/min on free; 3,000/min on paid; well-documented

known_agent_gotchas: API key is passed as query parameter — can be logged in access logs
OneCall API 3.0 requires subscription even for basic usage — check your plan
Weather data accuracy varies by location — rural areas have fewer stations
Forecast APIs use different endpoints than current weather
City search by name is imprecise — use latitude/longitude for accuracy
API v2.5 vs v3.0 have different features and pricing
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced but API key passed as query parameter (less secure than headers). No scope granularity. Low-risk use case — weather data is public. No notable compliance certifications.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key in query parameter is a security antipattern but low-risk for weather data. No compliance certs needed for public weather data.
